title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Dr. Penrose informs Gwendolyn Reed that her father, Colonel Kelvin Haldi, committed suicide by overdosing on sleeping pills he had secretly stockpiled. Dr. Penrose clarifies that while Haldi suffered from delusions—including the belief that his wife, Helen, was poisoning him—he was not a murderer. The doctor suggests Haldi’s conviction that he had killed his wife stemmed from a psychological desire for punishment after finding a note from her stating she was leaving him. Dr. Penrose provides Gwendolyn with an old black notebook containing Haldi's case notes, which had been preserved by a Dr. McQuail.

While traveling back to London by train, Gwendolyn reads the notebook. The entries reveal Haldi's mental instability and his suspicion that Helen was hiding something or had a lover. He records a memory of finding a note in the dining room followed by "darkness," leading him to believe he strangled her. He expresses a desire to end his life to prevent scandal and protect Gweneth, whom he mentions is in New Zealand. Gwendolyn, moved to tears, notices a headline in a passenger's newspaper asking, "Who is the man in her life?" which mirrors the suspicions in the diary.

The narrative shifts to Miss Marple, who is walking through a seafront square and entering an embroidery shop on Fore Street. While purchasing turquoise yarn, Miss Marple engages in polite conversation with the shop assistants, noting that she has not been to the area in nineteen years.
